 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	# AGENT IMPROVER: EVALUATION LOOP (AUTONOMOUS MODE)
     3	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	role: Agent Improvement Specialist using sk-improve-agent autonomously
     5	purpose: Integration-aware agent evaluation with 5-dimension scoring, deterministic benchmarks, and guarded promotion
     6	action: Scan integration surfaces, generate dynamic profiles, score candidates across 5 dimensions, reduce evidence, and decide keep/reject/promote
     7	
     8	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	# OPERATING MODE
    10	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    11	operating_mode:
    12	  workflow: iterative_evaluation_loop
    13	  workflow_compliance: MANDATORY
    14	  workflow_execution: autonomous
    15	  approvals: none
    16	  tracking: append_only_jsonl_ledger
    17	  validation: 5_dimension_deterministic_scoring
    18	  promotion: guarded_canonical_only
    19	  description: |
    20	    Executes the agent improvement loop without step-by-step approval.
    21	    Each iteration: scan integration -> propose candidate -> score across 5 dimensions -> benchmark -> reduce -> decide.
    22	    Stops when: max iterations reached, all dimensions plateau (3+ identical scores),
    23	    infra failures exceed threshold, or reducer signals shouldStop.
    24	
    25	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	# EVALUATION PHILOSOPHY
    27	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	evaluation_philosophy:
    29	  principle: "Prove improvement before mutation"
    30	  approach: "Integration-aware 5-dimension evaluation with deterministic scoring"
    31	  mandate: "Every candidate is bounded, every score is repeatable, every decision is auditable"
    32	
    33	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    34	# USER INPUTS
    35	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    36	user_inputs:
    37	  target_path: "[TARGET_PATH] - Path to the agent .md file to evaluate. REQUIRED. Any file in .opencode/agent/*.md."
    38	  target_profile: "[TARGET_PROFILE] - Profile ID: handover, context-prime, or dynamic. Auto-derived from target_path if not explicit."
    39	  scoring_mode: "[SCORING_MODE] - 'dynamic' for 5-dimension any-agent scoring, 'static' for legacy keyword profiles. Default: dynamic."
    40	  spec_folder: "[SPEC_FOLDER] - Spec folder path for improvement runtime. REQUIRED."
    41	  max_iterations: "[MAX_ITERATIONS] - Maximum loop iterations before forced stop. Default: 5."
    42	  execution_mode: "[EXECUTION_MODE] - AUTONOMOUS or INTERACTIVE. Derived from :auto/:confirm suffix."
    43	
    44	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45	# FIELD HANDLING
    46	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    47	field_handling:
    48	  # Field mapping: target_path -> target_profile (inferred), agent_name (basename without .md)
    49	  defaults:
    50	    scoring_mode_empty: "dynamic"
    51	    max_iterations_empty: "5"
    52	    target_profile_empty: "Derive from target_path: handover -> handover, context-prime -> context-prime, anything else -> dynamic"
    53	  validation:
    54	    target_path_required: true
    55	    spec_folder_required: true
    56	    target_path_must_exist: true
    57	    target_path_must_be_agent: "Must match .opencode/agent/*.md pattern"
    58	
    59	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    60	# CONTEXT LOADING
    61	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    62	context_loading:
    63	  trigger: "At workflow START, before phase_init"
    64	  mcp_integration:
    65	    tool: memory_search
    66	    note: "Call MCP tools directly - NEVER through Code Mode"
    67	    parameters: { query: "agent improvement context for {agent_name}", specFolder: "{spec_folder}", anchors: ['summary', 'decisions', 'state', 'improvement'] }
    68	  command_reference: "/memory:search"
    69	  when_to_skip: ["First-time evaluation of this agent", "User explicitly says 'skip context' or 'fresh start'", "No spec folder context exists"]
    70	  behavior:
    71	    if_context_found: "Surface prior improvement history, best-known scores, and dimensional trends before first iteration"
    72	    if_no_context: "Proceed to phase_init without delay"
    73	
    74	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    75	# SKILL REFERENCE
    76	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    77	skill_reference:
    78	  skill: sk-improve-agent
    79	  skill_md: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md
    80	  templates:
    81	    config: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json
    82	    strategy: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md
    83	    charter: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_charter.md
    84	    manifest: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/target_manifest.jsonc
    85	  scripts:
    86	    scanner: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs
    87	    profiler: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/generate-profile.cjs
    88	    scorer: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/score-candidate.cjs
    89	    benchmark: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/run-benchmark.cjs
    90	    journal: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs
    91	    reducer: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs
    92	
    93	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    94	# STATE PATHS
    95	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    96	state_paths:
    97	  runtime_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement"
    98	  candidates_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement/candidates"
    99	  config: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-config.json"
   100	  state_log: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   101	  strategy: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-strategy.md"
   102	  charter: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-charter.md"
   103	  manifest: "{spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc"
   104	  dashboard: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-dashboard.md"
   105	  registry: "{spec_folder}/improvement/experiment-registry.json"
   106	  integration_report: "{spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   107	  benchmark_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}"
   108	
   109	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   110	# WORKFLOW
   111	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112	workflow:
   113	  phase_init:
   114	    description: "Create runtime structure, scan integration surfaces, record baseline"
   115	    steps:
   116	      step_create_directories:
   117	        description: "Create improvement runtime directories"
   118	        command: "mkdir -p {spec_folder}/improvement/candidates {spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}"
   119	      step_scan_integration:
   120	        description: "Map all surfaces the target agent touches across the repo"
   121	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent={target_profile} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   122	      step_generate_profile:
   123	        description: "Generate dynamic scoring profile from the agent's own rules (skip if static profile)"
   124	        condition: "scoring_mode == 'dynamic'"
   125	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/generate-profile.cjs --agent={target_path} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/dynamic-profile.json"
   126	      step_copy_templates:
   127	        description: "Copy config, strategy, charter, and manifest templates into runtime root"
   128	        action: "Copy config, strategy, charter, and manifest templates into the runtime root"
   129	      step_record_baseline:
   130	        description: "Record baseline candidate state in append-only ledger"
   131	        action: "Append a baseline record for {target_path} into agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   132	      step_emit_journal_event:
   133	        description: "Emit session_start journal event before the first loop iteration begins"
   134	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_start --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"target\":\"{target_path}\",\"charter\":\"{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-charter.md\",\"startedAt\":\"{started_at}\"}'"
   135	
   136	  phase_loop:
   137	    description: "Iterative propose -> score -> benchmark -> reduce cycle"
   138	    repeat: true
   139	    max_repeats: "{max_iterations}"
   140	    steps:
   141	      step_scan_integration:
   142	        description: "Refresh integration surface map before each iteration"
   143	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent={target_profile} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   144	      step_generate_candidate:
   145	        description: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write one bounded candidate"
   146	        action: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write a new candidate into candidates/"
   147	      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_generated:
   148	        description: "Emit candidate_generated journal event after the candidate is written"
   149	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_generated --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"candidatePath\":\"{candidate_path}\"}'"
   150	      step_score_candidate:
   151	        description: "Score candidate across 5 dimensions (dynamic) or keyword checks (static)"
   152	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/score-candidate.cjs --candidate={candidate_path} --baseline={target_path} --manifest={spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc --profile={target_profile} --target={target_path} --output={score_output_path}"
   153	      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_scored:
   154	        description: "Emit candidate_scored journal event after scoring completes"
   155	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_scored --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"scoreOutputPath\":\"{score_output_path}\",\"weightedScore\":\"{weighted_score}\"}'"
   156	      step_run_benchmark:
   157	        description: "Run fixture tests against packet-local outputs"
   158	        action: "Run profile fixtures against packet-local outputs under benchmark-runs/{target_profile}/"
   159	      step_append_ledger:
   160	        description: "Record scored results in append-only JSONL ledger"
   161	        action: "Append prompt-score and benchmark records into agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   162	      step_reduce:
   163	        description: "Refresh dashboard, registry, and dimensional progress"
   164	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}/improvement"
   165	      step_stop_check:
   166	        description: "Evaluate stop conditions: dimension plateau, infra failure, weak benchmarks, max iterations"
   167	        action: "Stop if experiment-registry.json reports stopStatus.shouldStop: true"
   168	      step_emit_journal_event_gate_evaluation:
   169	        description: "Emit gate_evaluation journal event after stop-condition evaluation"
   170	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit gate_evaluation --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"gateName\":\"stop_check\",\"gateResult\":\"{stop_status}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\"}'"
   171	
   172	  phase_synthesis:
   173	    description: "After loop exits, summarize findings and recommend next action"
   174	    steps:
   175	      step_final_dashboard:
   176	        description: "Present final dashboard with per-dimension trends and stop reason"
   177	        action: "Read dashboard.md and present summary to operator"
   178	      step_recommendation:
   179	        description: "Recommend continue, promote, or stop based on reducer output"
   180	        action: "Check experiment-registry.json for bestPromptRecord and stopStatus"
   181	      step_emit_journal_event:
   182	        description: "Emit session_end journal event after synthesis completes"
   183	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_end --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\",\"sessionOutcome\":\"{session_outcome}\",\"endedAt\":\"{ended_at}\",\"totalIterations\":\"{total_iterations}\"}'"

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	# AGENT IMPROVER: EVALUATION LOOP (INTERACTIVE MODE)
     3	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	role: Agent Improvement Specialist using sk-improve-agent with operator approval
     5	purpose: Integration-aware agent evaluation with 5-dimension scoring, deterministic benchmarks, and guarded promotion
     6	action: Scan integration surfaces, generate dynamic profiles, score candidates across 5 dimensions, reduce evidence, and pause at each decision gate
     7	
     8	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	# OPERATING MODE
    10	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    11	operating_mode:
    12	  workflow: iterative_evaluation_loop
    13	  workflow_compliance: MANDATORY
    14	  workflow_execution: interactive
    15	  approvals: multi_gate
    16	  tracking: append_only_jsonl_ledger
    17	  validation: 5_dimension_deterministic_scoring
    18	  promotion: guarded_canonical_only
    19	  description: |
    20	    Executes the agent improvement loop with operator approval gates at key decision points.
    21	    Each iteration: scan integration -> propose candidate -> score across 5 dimensions -> benchmark -> reduce -> present gate.
    22	    The operator controls progression: approve each candidate generation, review scores before continuing,
    23	    and explicitly authorize any promotion. Stops when: operator chooses stop, max iterations reached,
    24	    all dimensions plateau (3+ identical scores), infra failures exceed threshold, or reducer signals shouldStop.
    25	
    26	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27	# EVALUATION PHILOSOPHY
    28	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    29	evaluation_philosophy:
    30	  principle: "Prove improvement before mutation"
    31	  approach: "Integration-aware 5-dimension evaluation with deterministic scoring"
    32	  mandate: "Every candidate is bounded, every score is repeatable, every decision is auditable"
    33	
    34	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    35	# USER INPUTS
    36	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	user_inputs:
    38	  target_path: "[TARGET_PATH] - Path to the agent .md file to evaluate. REQUIRED. Any file in .opencode/agent/*.md."
    39	  target_profile: "[TARGET_PROFILE] - Profile ID: handover, context-prime, or dynamic. Auto-derived from target_path if not explicit."
    40	  scoring_mode: "[SCORING_MODE] - 'dynamic' for 5-dimension any-agent scoring, 'static' for legacy keyword profiles. Default: dynamic."
    41	  spec_folder: "[SPEC_FOLDER] - Spec folder path for improvement runtime. REQUIRED."
    42	  max_iterations: "[MAX_ITERATIONS] - Maximum loop iterations before forced stop. Default: 5."
    43	  execution_mode: "[EXECUTION_MODE] - AUTONOMOUS or INTERACTIVE. Derived from :auto/:confirm suffix."
    44	
    45	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46	# FIELD HANDLING
    47	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    48	field_handling:
    49	  # Field mapping: target_path -> target_profile (inferred), agent_name (basename without .md)
    50	  defaults:
    51	    scoring_mode_empty: "dynamic"
    52	    max_iterations_empty: "5"
    53	    target_profile_empty: "Derive from target_path: handover -> handover, context-prime -> context-prime, anything else -> dynamic"
    54	  validation:
    55	    target_path_required: true
    56	    spec_folder_required: true
    57	    target_path_must_exist: true
    58	    target_path_must_be_agent: "Must match .opencode/agent/*.md pattern"
    59	
    60	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	# CONTEXT LOADING
    62	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    63	context_loading:
    64	  trigger: "At workflow START, before phase_init"
    65	  mcp_integration:
    66	    tool: memory_search
    67	    note: "Call MCP tools directly - NEVER through Code Mode"
    68	    parameters: { query: "agent improvement context for {agent_name}", specFolder: "{spec_folder}", anchors: ['summary', 'decisions', 'state', 'improvement'] }
    69	  command_reference: "/memory:search"
    70	  when_to_skip: ["First-time evaluation of this agent", "User explicitly says 'skip context' or 'fresh start'", "No spec folder context exists"]
    71	  behavior:
    72	    if_context_found: "Surface prior improvement history, best-known scores, and dimensional trends before first iteration"
    73	    if_no_context: "Proceed to phase_init without delay"
    74	  checkpoint:
    75	    question: "Prior improvement context found for this agent. Load it?"
    76	    options:
    77	      - { label: "A) Load context", description: "Review prior scores and improvement history" }
    78	      - { label: "B) Skip", description: "Start fresh without prior context" }
    79	
    80	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    81	# SKILL REFERENCE
    82	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    83	skill_reference:
    84	  skill: sk-improve-agent
    85	  skill_md: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md
    86	  templates:
    87	    config: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json
    88	    strategy: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md
    89	    charter: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_charter.md
    90	    manifest: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/target_manifest.jsonc
    91	  scripts:
    92	    scanner: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs
    93	    profiler: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/generate-profile.cjs
    94	    scorer: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/score-candidate.cjs
    95	    benchmark: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/run-benchmark.cjs
    96	    journal: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs
    97	    reducer: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs
    98	
    99	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   100	# STATE PATHS
   101	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   102	state_paths:
   103	  runtime_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement"
   104	  candidates_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement/candidates"
   105	  config: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-config.json"
   106	  state_log: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   107	  strategy: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-strategy.md"
   108	  charter: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-charter.md"
   109	  manifest: "{spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc"
   110	  dashboard: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-dashboard.md"
   111	  registry: "{spec_folder}/improvement/experiment-registry.json"
   112	  integration_report: "{spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   113	  benchmark_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}"
   114	
   115	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   116	# WORKFLOW
   117	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   118	workflow:
   119	  phase_init:
   120	    description: "Create runtime structure, scan integration surfaces, present init gate, record baseline"
   121	    steps:
   122	      step_create_directories:
   123	        description: "Create improvement runtime directories"
   124	        command: "mkdir -p {spec_folder}/improvement/candidates {spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}"
   125	      step_scan_integration:
   126	        description: "Map all surfaces the target agent touches across the repo"
   127	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent={target_profile} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   128	      step_generate_profile:
   129	        description: "Generate dynamic scoring profile from the agent's own rules (skip if static profile)"
   130	        condition: "scoring_mode == 'dynamic'"
   131	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/generate-profile.cjs --agent={target_path} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/dynamic-profile.json"
   132	      step_copy_templates:
   133	        description: "Copy config, strategy, charter, and manifest templates into runtime root"
   134	        action: "Copy config, strategy, charter, and manifest templates into the runtime root"
   135	      gate_init:
   136	        type: approval_gate
   137	        description: "Operator reviews initialization before recording baseline"
   138	        present: |
   139	          Improvement packet initialized for `{target_path}` using profile `{target_profile}`.
   140	          Integration scan complete: {integration_report_summary}.
   141	          Proposal-first mode is active.
   142	          Options:
   143	          A) Record baseline and continue to first iteration
   144	          B) Cancel
   145	      step_record_baseline:
   146	        description: "Record baseline candidate state in append-only ledger"
   147	        action: "Append a baseline record for {target_path} into agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   148	      step_emit_journal_event:
   149	        description: "Emit session_start journal event before the first loop iteration begins"
   150	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_start --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"target\":\"{target_path}\",\"charter\":\"{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-charter.md\",\"startedAt\":\"{started_at}\"}'"
   151	
   152	  phase_loop:
   153	    description: "Iterative propose -> score -> benchmark -> reduce cycle with operator gates"
   154	    repeat: true
   155	    max_repeats: "{max_iterations}"
   156	    steps:
   157	      step_scan_integration:
   158	        description: "Refresh integration surface map before each iteration"
   159	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent={target_profile} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   160	      gate_before_candidate:
   161	        type: approval_gate
   162	        description: "Operator authorizes candidate generation for this iteration"
   163	        present: |
   164	          Ready to generate candidate #{iteration} for `{target_path}`.
   165	          Options:
   166	          A) Generate candidate
   167	          B) Stop loop
   168	      step_generate_candidate:
   169	        description: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write one bounded candidate"
   170	        action: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write a new candidate into candidates/"
   171	      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_generated:
   172	        description: "Emit candidate_generated journal event after the candidate is written"
   173	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_generated --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"candidatePath\":\"{candidate_path}\"}'"
   174	      step_score_candidate:
   175	        description: "Score candidate across 5 dimensions (dynamic) or keyword checks (static)"
   176	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/score-candidate.cjs --candidate={candidate_path} --baseline={target_path} --manifest={spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc --profile={target_profile} --target={target_path} --output={score_output_path}"
   177	      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_scored:
   178	        description: "Emit candidate_scored journal event after scoring completes"
   179	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_scored --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"scoreOutputPath\":\"{score_output_path}\",\"weightedScore\":\"{weighted_score}\"}'"
   180	      step_run_benchmark:
   181	        description: "Run fixture tests against packet-local outputs"
   182	        action: "Run profile fixtures against packet-local outputs under benchmark-runs/{target_profile}/"
   183	      step_append_ledger:
   184	        description: "Record scored results in append-only JSONL ledger"
   185	        action: "Append prompt-score and benchmark records into agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   186	      step_reduce:
   187	        description: "Refresh dashboard, registry, and dimensional progress"
   188	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}/improvement"
   189	      gate_after_score:
   190	        type: approval_gate
   191	        description: "Operator reviews scores and decides next action"
   192	        present: |
   193	          Candidate scored. Review dimensional progress in dashboard.
   194	          Options:
   195	          A) Continue to next iteration
   196	          B) Promote accepted candidate (handover only, requires evidence)
   197	          C) Stop loop
   198	      step_emit_journal_event_gate_evaluation:
   199	        description: "Emit gate_evaluation journal event after the operator gate is evaluated"
   200	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit gate_evaluation --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"gateName\":\"gate_after_score\",\"gateDecision\":\"{gate_after_score}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\"}'"
   201	      step_promote_candidate:
   202	        description: "Promote the accepted candidate to canonical target (guarded, requires score evidence)"
   203	        condition: "gate_after_score == 'B' AND score_recommendation == 'candidate-better'"
   204	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/promote-candidate.cjs --candidate={candidate_path} --target={target_path} --score={score_output_path} --benchmark-report={benchmark_output_path} --repeatability-report={repeatability_output_path} --config={spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-config.json --manifest={spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc --archive-dir={spec_folder}/improvement/archive --approve"
   205	
   206	  phase_synthesis:
   207	    description: "After loop exits, summarize findings and recommend next action"
   208	    steps:
   209	      step_final_dashboard:
   210	        description: "Present final dashboard with per-dimension trends and stop reason"
   211	        action: "Read dashboard.md and present summary to operator"
   212	      step_recommendation:
   213	        description: "Recommend continue, promote, or stop based on reducer output"
   214	        action: "Check experiment-registry.json for bestPromptRecord and stopStatus"
   215	      step_emit_journal_event:
   216	        description: "Emit session_end journal event after synthesis completes"
   217	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_end --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\",\"sessionOutcome\":\"{session_outcome}\",\"endedAt\":\"{ended_at}\",\"totalIterations\":\"{total_iterations}\"}'"

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	# SMART SPECKIT: RESUME WORKFLOW (INTERACTIVE MODE)
     3	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	role: Expert Developer using Smart SpecKit for interactive session resume
     5	purpose: Resume previous work session with user confirmation at each step
     6	action: Detect session, confirm selection, load context, and present options
     7	
     8	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	# OPERATING MODE
    10	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    11	operating_mode:
    12	  workflow: utility
    13	  compliance: OPTIONAL
    14	  execution: interactive
    15	  approvals: step_by_step
    16	  tracking: session_state_detection
    17	  validation: checkpoint_based
    18	
    19	resume_philosophy:
    20	  principle: "Context continuity with user control and useful defaults"
    21	  approach: "Detect, recover just enough state, then let the user deepen only if needed"
    22	
    23	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24	# USER INPUTS
    25	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	user_inputs:
    27	  spec_folder: "[SPEC_FOLDER] - Spec folder path. Leave empty to auto-detect from most recent memory."
    28	
    29	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    30	# SESSION DETECTION
    31	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32	session_detection:
    33	  priority_order: ["CLI argument", "Deterministic candidate ranking", "Semantic memory search", "Trigger phrase matching", "Most recent indexed continuity support artifact by mtime"]
    34	  note: "Stateless architecture - no .spec-active marker file used. Interactive mode confirms ambiguous ranked detections."
    35	  deterministic_ranking:
    36	    roots: ["specs/", ".opencode/specs/"]
    37	    normalization: "Canonicalize alias roots before ranking (specs/ vs .opencode/specs/)"
    38	    filters: ["archive", "test", "fixture", "scratch"]
    39	    tie_breakers: ["artifact completeness", "recent activity", "stable path sort"]
    40	    confidence_contract:
    41	      high_confidence: "present top candidate for confirmation"
    42	      low_confidence_interactive: "present top 3 ranked candidates and require explicit user selection"
    43	  tier_details:
    44	    tier_1_cli:
    45	      method: "Parse $ARGUMENTS for spec folder path"
    46	      priority: "highest"
    47	    tier_1b_ranked_candidates:
    48	      method: "Enumerate candidate folders from both roots, normalize aliases, filter non-active folders, rank deterministically"
    49	      fallback: "tier_2"
    50	    tier_2_semantic:
    51	      method: "memory_list({ limit: 3, sortBy: 'updated_at' })"
    52	      fallback: "tier_3"
    53	    tier_3_triggers:
    54	      method: "memory_match_triggers({ prompt: 'resume work session context' })"
    55	      fallback: "tier_4"
    56	    tier_4_recent_artifacts:
    57	      method: "glob {.opencode/,}specs/**/{handover.md,implementation-summary.md,tasks.md} | filter active folders | sort by mtime | head -1"
    58	      fallback: "report no session"
    59	  phase_folder_detection:
    60	    trigger: "After spec folder resolved, check if it contains [0-9][0-9][0-9]-*/ child directories"
    61	    action: "If phase children found → list them with per-child tasks.md completion status → present phase selection UI before proceeding"
    62	    skip: "If --phase-folder was provided in $ARGUMENTS, skip detection and use the specified child directly"
    63	    detection_method_value: "phase-folder"
    64	  fallback: { on_no_memory: "Report no active session, suggest /spec_kit:complete" }
    65	
    66	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    67	# CONTEXT LOADING
    68	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69	context_loading_priority:
    70	  # 026: session_bootstrap() is preferred over plain memory_context() for interrupted-session recovery
    71	  # It provides graph-aware context injection and keeps recovery on the canonical resume/bootstrap ladder
    72	  order: ["Primary: handover.md (<24h old)", "Primary: _memory.continuity in implementation-summary.md", "Primary: supporting spec docs (tasks/checklist/plan/decision-record)", "Secondary: graph-metadata.json packet hints", "Secondary: session_bootstrap() or memory_context(mode: resume, profile: resume)", "Secondary: memory_search() with resume anchors"]
    73	  note: "Recover from the canonical packet first, then let the user choose whether MCP enrichment is needed"
    74	
    75	packet_graph_metadata:
    76	  role: "secondary packet hint"
    77	  allowed_signals: ["dependencies", "supersession", "related packets", "key files"]
    78	  constraint: "Never replace the canonical handover/continuity/spec-doc recovery ladder."
    79	
    80	memory_loading:
    81	  mode: interactive
    82	  behavior: "Check handover.md (<24h) first, then run session_bootstrap() or memory_context() in resume mode with profile=resume and only ask for extra memory when the recovery packet is still missing something important"
    83	  options:
    84	    - { id: A, label: "Fast resume", description: "Just enough context to continue safely" }
    85	    - { id: B, label: "Fill missing next step / blockers", description: "Target only the missing recovery gaps" }
    86	    - { id: C, label: "Deep context", description: "Load up to 3 recent files for a fuller picture" }
    87	    - { id: D, label: "Artifacts only", description: "Use handover, _memory.continuity, and supporting spec docs without extra MCP context" }
    88	  mcp_integration:
    89	    tool: memory_context
    90	    note: "Call MCP tools directly - NEVER through Code Mode"
    91	    parameters: { input: "resume previous work continue session", mode: "resume", profile: "resume", specFolder: "{spec_folder_path}", includeContent: true }
    92	
    93	smart_memory_logic:
    94	  objective: "Recover enough state to continue safely, then let the user choose whether to deepen context"
    95	  essentials: ["current phase or task", "last confirmed action", "next safe action", "blockers or none", "relevant artifact or file"]
    96	  sufficiency_rule: "Stop and present the resume brief once next safe action is known plus any two supporting essentials"
    97	  gap_fill_order:
    98	    - "If current phase or task is missing, inspect tasks.md/checklist.md/handover.md"
    99	    - "If next safe action is missing, use session_bootstrap() or memory_context(mode: resume, profile: resume) then targeted memory_search on next-steps/state"
   100	    - "If blockers are missing, target blockers/summary"
   101	    - "If a concrete starting point is missing, use tasks.md or implementation-summary.md before loading more memory"
   102	    - "If ambiguity remains, show the uncertainty and ask the user which direction to investigate"
   103	
   104	checkpoint_options:
   105	  session_selection:
   106	    - { label: "Confirm", description: "This is the correct spec folder" }
   107	    - { label: "Select Different", description: "Choose a different spec folder" }
   108	    - { label: "Cancel", description: "Cancel resume operation" }
   109	  continuation:
   110	    - { label: "Continue from pending work", description: "Pick up where you left off" }
   111	    - { label: "Run /spec_kit:implement", description: "Execute remaining tasks" }
   112	    - { label: "Just chat", description: "Work on it manually" }
   113	
   114	stale_session_detection:
   115	  check: "last_modified > 7 days"
   116	  interactive_behavior:
   117	    options:
   118	      - { label: "Resume anyway", description: "Load context and continue" }
   119	      - { label: "Fresh start", description: "Keep artifacts, restart workflow" }
   120	      - { label: "Review first", description: "Show what changed in codebase" }
   121	      - { label: "Cancel", description: "Do not resume" }
   122	
   123	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   124	# CONFIDENCE & CLARIFICATION FRAMEWORK
   125	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   126	confidence_framework:
   127	  thresholds:
   128	    high: { range: "80-100%", action: "Proceed with context loading and resume" }
   129	    medium: { range: "40-79%", action: "Proceed with caution - present stale session options" }
   130	    low: { range: "0-39%", action: "STOP - Ask user about context reliability and avoid guessing the next step" }
   131	  scoring_weights: { session_recency: 0.40, artifact_completeness: 0.30, memory_availability: 0.20, codebase_changes: 0.10 }
   132	  reply_format: ["Confidence: NN%", "Context source: handover|memory", "Now", "Last confirmed", "Next safe action"]
   133	  escalation: { timebox_minutes: 5, failed_threshold: 2, action: "Present options to user with current findings" }
   134	
   135	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   136	# WORKFLOW
   137	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   138	workflow:
   139	  step_1_session_detection:
   140	    purpose: Detect active session from CLI argument or recent canonical spec artifacts
   141	    activities:
   142	      - Check CLI argument for spec folder
   143	      - If no argument, build deterministic candidates from `specs/` and `.opencode/specs/`
   144	      - Canonicalize aliases and filter archive/test/fixture/scratch paths
   145	      - Rank candidates deterministically with stable tie-breakers
   146	      - If low confidence, present top-ranked candidates for explicit selection
   147	      - If no ranked candidate, continue to semantic/memory fallback
   148	      - Validate resolved spec folder exists
   149	    outputs: { spec_folder: detected_path, detection_method: argument_or_memory }
   150	    validation: session_detected
   151	    checkpoint:
   152	      question: "Found session. Is this the correct spec folder?"
   153	      options:
   154	        - { label: "Confirm", description: "This is the correct spec folder" }
   155	        - { label: "Select Different", description: "Choose a different spec folder" }
   156	        - { label: "Cancel", description: "Cancel resume operation" }
   157	
   158	  step_2_memory_selection:
   159	    purpose: Recover the default resume packet first, then let the user deepen only where the packet is still weak
   160	    activities:
   161	      - Check for handover.md in spec_folder (<24h old)
   162	      - If recent handover exists, load and display contents
   163	      - Attempt session_bootstrap() or memory_context() with mode="resume" and profile="resume" for the resolved spec folder
   164	      - Extract Resume Essentials (current phase/task, last confirmed action, next safe action, blockers, relevant artifact/file)
   165	      - Present targeted depth options instead of raw file-loading choices
   166	      - Present numbered options to user, wait for selection
   167	    context_priority: ["handover.md", "_memory.continuity", "supporting spec docs", "session_bootstrap()", "memory_context(mode: resume, profile: resume)", "memory_search() with resume anchors"]
   168	    outputs: { handover_loaded: "true|false", primary_recovery_loaded: "true|false", selected_memory: user_choice, load_count: number }
   169	    checkpoint:
   170	      question: "Do you want more context, or is the resume brief already enough?"
   171	      note: "Prefer the smallest context packet that makes the next action clear"
   172	      options:
   173	        - { label: "A) Fast resume", description: "Current resume brief is enough" }
   174	        - { label: "B) Fill missing next step / blockers", description: "Target only the missing recovery gaps" }
   175	        - { label: "C) Deep context", description: "Load up to 3 recent files for a fuller picture" }
   176	        - { label: "D) Artifacts only", description: "Continue with the canonical packet and no extra MCP context" }
   177	
   178	  step_3_load_memory:
   179	    purpose: Fill specific recovery gaps instead of broad-loading memory by default
   180	    activities:
   181	      - If the user chose Fast resume, keep the current recovery packet and do not load more
   182	      - If the user chose Fill missing next step / blockers, run targeted anchored memory_search() only for those gaps
   183	      - If the user chose Deep context, use `session_bootstrap()`/`memory_context()` plus targeted `memory_search()` to enrich the canonical packet
   184	      - If the user chose Artifacts only, rely on handover, `_memory.continuity`, and supporting spec docs and skip supplemental MCP context
   185	      - Merge supplemental results with handover and primary recovery context
   186	      - Extract Project State Snapshot (Phase, Active File, Last confirmed action, Next safe action, Blockers)
   187	      - Parse and summarize combined context
   188	    mcp_tool:
   189	      name: memory_context
   190	      note: "Call directly - NEVER through Code Mode. Use it only after the canonical packet is still thin; use anchored memory_search() only when more detail is still needed."
   191	      parameters: { input: "resume previous work continue session", mode: "resume", profile: "resume", specFolder: "{detected_spec_folder}", includeContent: true }
   192	    outputs: { memory_loaded: true, context_source: "handover|continuity|spec_docs|memory_context|memory_search|combined", context_summary: extracted_context, resume_packet: "now|last|next|blockers|artifact" }
   193	    validation: context_loaded
   194	
   195	  step_4_calculate_progress:
   196	    purpose: Calculate task and checklist progress
   197	    activities: [Parse tasks.md for completion status, Parse checklist.md for verification status, Calculate percentages, Identify pending items]
   198	    outputs: { tasks_progress: percentage, checklist_progress: percentage, pending_items: list }
   199	    validation: progress_calculated
   200	
   201	  step_5_present_resume:
   202	    purpose: Display a concise resume brief and offer continuation options
   203	    activities: [Display spec folder and last activity, Show progress bars, Show Now/Last confirmed/Next safe action/Blockers, Cite the artifact or task that justifies the recommendation, Present continuation options]
   204	    outputs: { resume_summary: displayed }
   205	    validation: resume_complete
   206	    checkpoint:
   207	      question: "Ready to continue. What would you like to do?"
   208	      options:
   209	        - { label: "Continue from pending work", description: "Pick up where you left off" }
   210	        - { label: "Run /spec_kit:implement", description: "Execute remaining tasks" }
   211	        - { label: "Just chat", description: "Work on it manually" }
   212	
   213	stale_session:
   214	  threshold_days: 7
   215	  detection: { check: "last_modified > 7 days", action: "Present warning and options" }
   216	  # 026: stale sessions score low on the confidence framework (session_recency weight = 0.40)
   217	  # Apply dual-threshold check: if confidence < 0.70 OR uncertainty > 0.35, present options
   218	  interactive_behavior:
   219	    options:
   220	      - { label: "Resume anyway", description: "Load context and continue" }
   221	      - { label: "Fresh start", description: "Keep artifacts, restart workflow" }
   222	      - { label: "Review first", description: "Show what changed" }
   223	      - { label: "Cancel", description: "Do not resume" }
   224	
   225	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   226	# WORKFLOW TERMINATION
   227	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   228	termination:
   229	  after_step: 5
   230	  message: "Session resume complete. Ready to continue work."
   231	  next_steps:
   232	    - "Continue from pending work"
   233	    - "Review implementation summary"
   234	    - "Run /spec_kit:implement if tasks remain"
   235	
   236	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   237	# INTERACTIVE EXECUTION GUIDANCE
   238	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   239	interactive_execution:
   240	  principle: "Execute resume with user approval at each checkpoint"
   241	  checkpoint_behavior: [Complete step activities, Present results summary, Present options for selection, Wait for response, Process feedback, Proceed or redirect as directed]
   242	  user_feedback_handling: { confirm: "Proceed to next step", select_different: "Show folder selection options", skip: "Proceed without loading memory", cancel: "Abort resume operation" }
   243	
   244	crash_recovery:
   245	  command_reference: "/spec_kit:resume"
   246	  description: "Recovery is handled inside the resume workflow through handover.md, then _memory.continuity, then supporting spec docs, with MCP enrichment only when the canonical packet is thin"
   247	  when_to_use: ["Session interrupted unexpectedly", "Context compaction detected", "Need to recover without handover.md"]
   248	  integration_with_resume:
   249	    relationship: "/spec_kit:resume = standard continuation + interrupted-session recovery"
   250	    recommendation: "Use /spec_kit:resume for both ordinary continuation and crash recovery"
   251	
   252	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   253	# ERROR RECOVERY
   254	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   255	error_recovery:
   256	  no_active_session: "Report no session, suggest /spec_kit:complete"
   257	  spec_folder_missing: "Report not found, ask user to specify or create"
   258	  memory_search_failed: { action: "Report that supplemental anchored recovery failed", fallback: "Offer to continue with the current /spec_kit:resume context or start fresh" }
   259	  stale_session_detected: "Present stale session options to user"
   260	  user_cancels: "Acknowledge cancellation, exit gracefully"
   261	  crash_detected: "Continue with the built-in resume recovery chain"
   262	  insufficient_context: "Show what is known, call out what is still unclear, and ask which missing area to investigate"
   263	
   264	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   265	# RULES
   266	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   267	rules:
   268	  ALWAYS:
   269	  - detect_session_before_loading
   270	  - confirm_spec_folder_with_user
   271	  - present_memory_options
   272	  - calculate_progress_from_artifacts
   273	  - display_clear_summary
   274	  - use_mcp_tools_directly
   275	  - respect_user_selections
   276	  - stop_loading_when_context_is_sufficient
   277	  - recommend_one_safe_next_action
   278	  NEVER:
   279	  - auto_load_without_confirmation
   280	  - proceed_without_user_approval
   281	  - proceed_without_valid_spec_folder
   282	  - call_mcp_through_code_mode
   283	  - ignore_user_cancellation
   284	  - dump_extra_memory_when_the_next_step_is_already_clear
   285	  - guess_missing_state_when_confidence_is_low

.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:263:All journal emission is orchestrator-only (ADR-001). The journal (`improvement-journal.jsonl`) is an append-only JSONL file capturing lifecycle events. Separate from the existing `agent-improvement-state.jsonl` which tracks proposal/evaluation data.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:265:**Script**: `scripts/improvement-journal.cjs`
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:267:Event types: `session_start`, `session_initialized`, `integration_scanned`, `candidate_generated`, `candidate_scored`, `benchmark_completed`, `gate_evaluation`, `legal_stop_evaluated`, `blocked_stop`, `promotion_attempt`, `promotion_result`, `rollback`, `rollback_result`, `trade_off_detected`, `mutation_proposed`, `mutation_outcome`, `session_ended`, `session_end`
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:281:Failed gates persist `blockedStop` with full gate results in the journal.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:285:Sessions support a single lineage mode today: `new`. Every invocation of the `/improve:agent` workflow starts a fresh session with a new session id and generation 1. Multi-generation lineage modes (`resume`, `restart`, `fork`, `completed-continue`) were described in earlier drafts but have no shipped runtime wiring in the improve-agent workflow, reducer, or journal consumer.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:289:If the long-form lineage feature is implemented later, it will arrive with first-class event emission in `improve_improve-agent_{auto,confirm}.yaml`, reducer ancestry handling in `sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs`, and replay fixtures. Until then, treat every session as a standalone evaluation.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:324:Journal emission is orchestrator-only. The target agent being evaluated never writes journal rows directly; only the visible YAML workflow or an operator-side wrapper invokes `scripts/improvement-journal.cjs`.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:329:node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit <eventType> --journal <journal_path> --details '<json>'
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:332:The helper validates event type plus `session_end` or `session_ended` details, and the CLI entrypoint stores boundary context under `details`. Top-level `iteration` and `candidateId` fields are available only through the JS API, not through the CLI wrapper used by the YAML workflows.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:338:| `session_start` | Once after baseline setup is recorded and before the first loop iteration begins | `session_start` | `sessionId`, `target`, `charter`, `startedAt` |
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:339:| `iteration_boundary` | On every iteration after candidate generation, after candidate scoring, and after gate evaluation | `candidate_generated`, `candidate_scored`, `gate_evaluation` | Per-iteration context such as `sessionId`, `iteration`, `candidateId`, `candidatePath`, `scoreOutputPath`, `weightedScore`, and gate decision details |
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:340:| `session_end` | Once after synthesis completes or the session reaches a terminal stop | `session_end` | `stopReason`, `sessionOutcome`, `endedAt`, `totalIterations` |
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:344:`improvement-journal.cjs` currently exports and validates the following enums:
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:346:- `STOP_REASONS`: `converged`, `maxIterationsReached`, `blockedStop`, `manualStop`, `error`, `stuckRecovery`
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:353:- Auto mode emits `session_start` after `step_record_baseline`, then emits `candidate_generated`, `candidate_scored`, and `gate_evaluation` inside each loop iteration, and finally emits `session_end` after synthesis.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:354:- Confirm mode mirrors the same boundaries, with `gate_evaluation` emitted after the operator-facing approval gate is resolved.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:355:- Operators invoking the helper manually must use the same boundary order so replay and reducer consumers see a consistent journal shape.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:359:The reducer is the consumer for replay artifacts on refresh. Every `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` pass now attempts to read:
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:361:- `improvement-journal.jsonl`
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:369:ADR-002 is implemented in the reducer via replay consumers instead of a separate orchestrator-only synthesis step. During each refresh pass, `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` now reads the following artifacts when present:
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:371:- `improvement-journal.jsonl` to summarize last session boundaries, total replayed events, per-event counts, and terminal `stopReason` / `sessionOutcome`
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:377:- `journalSummary`
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:383:The dashboard now also includes a dedicated **Sample Quality** section. This separates replay/stability sample sufficiency from benchmark failures so operators can tell the difference between a true regression and an iteration that simply lacked enough data for trade-off or replay-stability trust.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:443:| `scripts/improvement-journal.cjs` | Append-only JSONL event emitter for improvement session audit journals |
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:447:| `scripts/benchmark-stability.cjs` | Benchmark replay stability measurement and advisory weight optimization |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:269:- `resume` — continue the active lineage; appends a typed `resumed` JSONL event with `sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `continuedFromRun`, `generation`, `archivedPath` (null), `timestamp`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:270:- `restart` — archive the existing `review/` tree under `review_archive/{timestamp}/`, mint a fresh `sessionId`, increment `generation`, and append a typed `restarted` JSONL event with the same field set plus a non-null `archivedPath`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:273:- `fork` — earlier drafts described a sibling-lineage branch; the workflow no longer exposes this option
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:274:- `completed-continue` — earlier drafts described snapshotting `review-report-v{generation}.md`; not runtime-wired
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:451:- **Continuity**: `/spec_kit:resume` is the operator-facing recovery surface; canonical packet continuity is written via `generate-context.js`

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:478:| `/spec_kit:resume` | Canonical recovery surface before resuming or extending an active review packet |
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:51:- `resume` — continue the active lineage; appends a typed `resumed` JSONL event with `sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `continuedFromRun`, `generation`, `archivedPath` (null), and `timestamp`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:52:- `restart` — archive the existing `research/` tree under `research_archive/{timestamp}/`, mint a fresh `sessionId`, increment `generation`, and append a typed `restarted` JSONL event with the same field set plus a non-null `archivedPath`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:55:- `fork` — earlier drafts described a sibling-lineage branch; the workflow no longer exposes this as an option
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:56:- `completed-continue` — earlier drafts described snapshotting the prior synthesis as immutable `synthesis-v{generation}.md`; not runtime-wired
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:87:    "STATE": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["state file", "JSONL", "strategy", "resume", "auto-resume"]},
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:94:    "STATE": {"pick up where": 1.5, "continue from": 1.4, "resume": 1.8},
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:350:- **Continuity**: `/spec_kit:resume` is the operator-facing recovery surface; canonical packet continuity is written via `generate-context.js`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:357:  /spec_kit:resume
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:379:| `/spec_kit:resume` | Canonical recovery surface before resuming or extending an active packet |
.opencode/command/spec_kit/deep-research.md:289:- Promotion is advisory-only until replay fixtures and behavioral suites exist

I’ve got a few concrete integrity risks emerging already, especially around completed-session handling and journal completeness. I’m checking the helper scripts those YAMLs call now so I can tell whether these are real runtime breaks or just doc-level quirks.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
// ║ Improvement Journal — Append-Only Audit Journal for Improvement Sessions║
// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
'use strict';

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 1. IMPORTS
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
const fs = require('node:fs');
const path = require('node:path');

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 2. CONSTANTS — Stop-Reason Taxonomy & Session Outcomes
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Stop reasons explain WHY a session terminated.
 * Research finding (P0): do not overload stopReason with outcome semantics.
 * @type {Readonly<Record<string, string>>}
 */
const STOP_REASONS = Object.freeze({
  converged: 'converged',
  maxIterationsReached: 'maxIterationsReached',
  blockedStop: 'blockedStop',
  manualStop: 'manualStop',
  error: 'error',
  stuckRecovery: 'stuckRecovery',
});

/**
 * Session outcomes explain WHAT happened to the candidate.
 * Separate from stopReason per research finding (P0).
 * @type {Readonly<Record<string, string>>}
 */
const SESSION_OUTCOMES = Object.freeze({
  keptBaseline: 'keptBaseline',
  promoted: 'promoted',
  rolledBack: 'rolledBack',
  advisoryOnly: 'advisoryOnly',
});

/**
 * Valid event types for the improvement journal.
 * Journal captures lifecycle events and stop decisions (ADR-001: orchestrator only).
 * @type {Readonly<string[]>}
 */
const VALID_EVENT_TYPES = Object.freeze([
  'session_start',
  'session_initialized',
  'integration_scanned',
  'candidate_generated',
  'candidate_scored',
  'benchmark_completed',
  'gate_evaluation',
  'legal_stop_evaluated',
  'blocked_stop',
  'promotion_attempt',
  'promotion_attempted',
  'promotion_result',
  'rollback',
  'rollback_result',
  'trade_off_detected',
  'mutation_proposed',
  'mutation_outcome',
  'session_ended',
  'session_end',
]);

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 3. HELPERS
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Validate that an event object has the required fields and a valid event type.
 * @param {object} event - Event to validate
 * @returns {{ valid: boolean, errors: string[] }}
 */
function validateEvent(event) {
  const errors = [];

  if (!event || typeof event !== 'object') {
    return { valid: false, errors: ['Event must be a non-null object'] };
  }

  if (!event.eventType || typeof event.eventType !== 'string') {
    errors.push('Event must have a string eventType');
  } else if (!VALID_EVENT_TYPES.includes(event.eventType)) {
    errors.push(`Invalid eventType: "${event.eventType}". Valid types: ${VALID_EVENT_TYPES.join(', ')}`);
  }

  if (event.eventType === 'session_ended' || event.eventType === 'session_end') {
    if (!event.details || !event.details.stopReason) {
      errors.push('session_ended/session_end events MUST include details.stopReason');
    } else if (!Object.values(STOP_REASONS).includes(event.details.stopReason)) {
      errors.push(`Invalid stopReason: "${event.details.stopReason}". Valid reasons: ${Object.values(STOP_REASONS).join(', ')}`);
    }
    if (!event.details || !event.details.sessionOutcome) {
      errors.push('session_ended/session_end events MUST include details.sessionOutcome');
    } else if (!Object.values(SESSION_OUTCOMES).includes(event.details.sessionOutcome)) {
      errors.push(`Invalid sessionOutcome: "${event.details.sessionOutcome}". Valid outcomes: ${Object.values(SESSION_OUTCOMES).join(', ')}`);
    }
  }

  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
}

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 4. CORE API
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Append an event to the improvement journal (append-only JSONL).
 * ADR-001: Journal emission is orchestrator-only; this function is the single write point.
 *
 * @param {string} journalPath - Path to the improvement-journal.jsonl file
 * @param {object} event - Event object with { eventType, iteration?, candidateId?, details? }
 * @returns {{ success: boolean, errors?: string[] }}
 */
function emitEvent(journalPath, event) {
  const enrichedEvent = {
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    ...event,
  };

  const validation = validateEvent(enrichedEvent);
  if (!validation.valid) {
    return { success: false, errors: validation.errors };
  }

  // Ensure directory exists
  const dir = path.dirname(journalPath);
  fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });

  // Append-only: use 'a' flag (NFR-R01: survives process restart, no in-memory buffering)
  const line = JSON.stringify(enrichedEvent) + '\n';
  fs.appendFileSync(journalPath, line, 'utf8');

  return { success: true };
}

/**
 * Read all events from a journal file.
 * Used for resume/replay semantics (REQ-AI-003).
 *
 * @param {string} journalPath - Path to the improvement-journal.jsonl file
 * @returns {object[]} Array of parsed event objects
 */
function readJournal(journalPath) {
  try {
    const content = fs.readFileSync(journalPath, 'utf8');
    return content
      .split('\n')
      .map((line) => line.trim())
      .filter(Boolean)
      .flatMap((line) => {
        try {
          return [JSON.parse(line)];
        } catch (_err) {
          return [];
        }
      });
  } catch (_err) {
    return [];
  }
}

/**
 * Get the last completed iteration number from a journal.
 * Used for resume semantics — continuedFromIteration field.
 *
 * @param {string} journalPath - Path to the improvement-journal.jsonl file
 * @returns {number} Last completed iteration number, or 0 if none
 */
function getLastIteration(journalPath) {
  const events = readJournal(journalPath);
  let maxIteration = 0;
  for (const event of events) {
    if (typeof event.iteration === 'number' && event.iteration > maxIteration) {
      maxIteration = event.iteration;
    }
  }
  return maxIteration;
}

/**
 * Get the stop reason from the last session_ended event.
 *
 * @param {string} journalPath - Path to the improvement-journal.jsonl file
 * @returns {{ stopReason: string|null, sessionOutcome: string|null }}
 */
function getSessionResult(journalPath) {
  const events = readJournal(journalPath);
  const endEvents = events.filter(
    (e) => e.eventType === 'session_ended' || e.eventType === 'session_end'
  );
  if (endEvents.length === 0) {
    return { stopReason: null, sessionOutcome: null };
  }
  const last = endEvents[endEvents.length - 1];
  return {
    stopReason: (last.details && last.details.stopReason) || null,
    sessionOutcome: (last.details && last.details.sessionOutcome) || null,
  };
}

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 5. EXPORTS
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

module.exports = {
  STOP_REASONS,
  SESSION_OUTCOMES,
  VALID_EVENT_TYPES,
  validateEvent,
  emitEvent,
  readJournal,
  getLastIteration,
  getSessionResult,
};

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 6. CLI ENTRYPOINT
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

if (require.main === module) {
  const args = process.argv.slice(2);

  function findArg(name) {
    const prefix = `--${name}=`;
    for (const arg of args) {
      if (arg.startsWith(prefix)) {
        return arg.slice(prefix.length);
      }
    }
    const idx = args.indexOf(`--${name}`);
    if (idx !== -1 && idx + 1 < args.length && !args[idx + 1].startsWith('--')) {
      return args[idx + 1];
    }
    return undefined;
  }

  const readPath = findArg('read');
  const emitType = findArg('emit');
  const journalPath = findArg('journal');
  const detailsRaw = findArg('details');

    // --read <path>: Read and dump journal as JSON array
    const events = readJournal(readPath);
    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(events, null, 2) + '\n');
  } else if (emitType && journalPath) {
    // --emit <eventType> --journal <path> [--details <json>]
    let details = {};
    if (detailsRaw) {
      try {
        details = JSON.parse(detailsRaw);
      } catch (err) {
        process.stderr.write(`Failed to parse --details JSON: ${err.message}\n`);
        process.exit(2);
      }

'use strict';

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 1. IMPORTS
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

const fs = require('node:fs');
const path = require('node:path');

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 2. HELPERS
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function readUtf8(filePath) {
  return fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
}

function readOptionalUtf8(filePath) {
  try {
    return readUtf8(filePath);
  } catch (_error) {
    return null;
  }
}

function parseJson(content, fallback) {
  try {
    return JSON.parse(content);
  } catch (_error) {
    return fallback;
  }
}

function readOptionalJson(filePath) {
  const content = readOptionalUtf8(filePath);
  if (content === null) {
    return null;
  }
  return parseJson(content, null);
}

function writeUtf8(filePath, content) {
  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });
  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, content, 'utf8');
}

function parseJsonl(content) {
  return content
    .split('\n')
    .map((line) => line.trim())
    .filter(Boolean)
    .flatMap((line) => {
      try {
        return [JSON.parse(line)];
      } catch (_error) {
        return [];
      }
    });
}

function isPlainObject(value) {
  return Boolean(value) && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
}

function isFiniteNumber(value) {
  return typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value);
}

function sortObjectKeys(value) {
  return Object.fromEntries(
    Object.entries(value).sort((left, right) => left[0].localeCompare(right[0]))
  );
}

function findNestedState(value, expectedState, seen = new Set()) {
  if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') {
    return null;
  }

  if (seen.has(value)) {
    return null;
  }
  seen.add(value);

  if (isPlainObject(value) && value.state === expectedState) {
    return value;
  }

  const entries = Array.isArray(value) ? value : Object.values(value);
  for (const entry of entries) {
    const found = findNestedState(entry, expectedState, seen);
    if (found) {
      return found;
    }
  }

  return null;
}

function inferRun(record, statePayload, fallbackIndex) {
  const candidates = [
    statePayload?.run,
    statePayload?.iteration,
    statePayload?.runNumber,
    record?.run,
    record?.iteration,
    record?.runNumber,
    record?.label,
    fallbackIndex + 1,
  ];

  for (const candidate of candidates) {
    if (typeof candidate === 'number' && Number.isFinite(candidate)) {
      return candidate;
    }
    if (typeof candidate === 'string' && candidate.trim()) {
      return candidate;
    }
  }

  return fallbackIndex + 1;
}

function extractInsufficientDataIteration(record, fallbackIndex) {
  const statePayload = findNestedState(record, 'insufficientData');
  if (
    !statePayload ||
    typeof statePayload.dataPoints !== 'number' ||
    typeof statePayload.minRequired !== 'number'
  ) {
    return null;
  }

  return {
    run: inferRun(record, statePayload, fallbackIndex),
    dataPoints: statePayload.dataPoints,
    minRequired: statePayload.minRequired,
  };
}

function extractInsufficientSampleIteration(record, fallbackIndex) {
  const statePayload = findNestedState(record, 'insufficientSample');
  if (
    !statePayload ||
    typeof statePayload.replayCount !== 'number' ||
    typeof statePayload.minRequired !== 'number'
  ) {
    return null;
  }

  return {
    run: inferRun(record, statePayload, fallbackIndex),
    replayCount: statePayload.replayCount,
    minRequired: statePayload.minRequired,
  };
}

function inferProfileId(record) {
  if (record.profileId) {
    return record.profileId;
  }
  return 'dynamic';
}

function inferFamily(record, profileId) {
  if (record.family) return record.family;
  return profileId;
}

function buildJournalSummary(filePath) {
  const content = readOptionalUtf8(filePath);
  if (content === null) {
    return null;
  }

  const events = parseJsonl(content).filter((event) => isPlainObject(event));
  const eventTypeCounts = {};
  let lastSessionStart = null;
  let lastSessionEnd = null;
  let stopReason = null;
  let sessionOutcome = null;

  for (const event of events) {
    const eventType = typeof event.eventType === 'string' ? event.eventType : null;
    const timestamp = typeof event.timestamp === 'string' ? event.timestamp : null;
    const details = isPlainObject(event.details) ? event.details : {};

    if (eventType) {
      eventTypeCounts[eventType] = (eventTypeCounts[eventType] || 0) + 1;
    }

    if (eventType === 'session_start' && timestamp) {
      lastSessionStart = timestamp;
    }

    if ((eventType === 'session_end' || eventType === 'session_ended') && timestamp) {
      lastSessionEnd = timestamp;
      stopReason =
        typeof details.stopReason === 'string' && details.stopReason.trim()
          ? details.stopReason
          : stopReason;
      sessionOutcome =
        typeof details.sessionOutcome === 'string' && details.sessionOutcome.trim()
          ? details.sessionOutcome
          : sessionOutcome;
    }
  }

  return {
    lastSessionStart,
    lastSessionEnd,
    totalEvents: events.length,
    eventTypeCounts: sortObjectKeys(eventTypeCounts),
    stopReason,
    sessionOutcome,
  };
}

function buildCandidateLineageSummary(filePath) {
  const data = readOptionalJson(filePath);
  if (!isPlainObject(data) || !Array.isArray(data.nodes)) {
    return null;
  }

  const nodes = data.nodes.filter((node) => isPlainObject(node) && typeof node.id === 'string');

      return 0;
    }
    if (memo.has(nodeId)) {
      return memo.get(nodeId);
    }
    if (trail.has(nodeId)) {
      return 0;
